{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Digital Marketing Executive",
  "description": "DIY Africans is a Nigerian furniture and home goods company creating functional, affordable, and locally manufactured products for the next generation of African households.&nbsp;

We combine local manufacturing, digital commerce, and design-led products to make quality home goods more accessible. As we enter our next phase of growth, we&#39;re hiring a Digital Marketing Executive to drive awareness, generate leads, support sales, and strengthen our digital presence.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Digital Marketing Executive

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The Opportunity


	As the Digital Marketing Executive you will own DIY Africans&#39; marketing efforts from strategy through execution and operate the role with a commercial marketing mindset with an understanding that marketing drives business growth.
	Confidently planning campaigns, creating content, analyzing performance, and optimizing results in a fast-paced startup environment.


Success looks like:


	Increased brand visibility and a steady flow of qualified leads
	Stronger customer acquisition and online sales growth
	A more consistent, recognizable digital presence
	Product and lifestyle content that reflects the brand&#39;s quality
	Reporting: You will&nbsp;work directly with leadership rather than into an existing marketing team - ideal for someone who wants ownership early.


Key Responsibilities
Digital Marketing &amp; Growth:


	Plan and execute digital marketing campaigns across paid and organic channels
	Manage and optimize Meta advertising campaigns (Facebook &amp; Instagram)
	Generate and nurture sales leads through to conversion
	Monitor campaign performance and recommend improvements


Content Creation &amp; Social Media:


	Develop engaging content for social media platforms
	Participate in product and lifestyle content shoots
	Build content calendars and posting schedules
	Manage day-to-day social media activity, including customer enquiries and engagement


Brand Building


	Strengthen DIY Africans&#39; digital presence
	Contribute ideas for brand growth and customer retention


Reporting &amp; Analytics


	Track marketing performance metrics
	Prepare weekly and monthly reports
	Monitor lead generation and conversion performance
	Provide insights and recommendations to leadership


Requirements
Must Have


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree with 2&ndash;4 years&#39; experience in Digital Marketing, Growth Marketing, or Social Media Management.
	Hands-on experience managing Meta Ads (Facebook &amp; Instagram), with budget accountability
	Strong content creation skills
	Good understanding of customer acquisition and lead generation
	Familiarity with Canva, CapCut, or similar creative tools
	Strong written and verbal communication skills


Preferred:


	SEO knowledge
	Google Business Profile experience
	Basic website management experience
	Experience working with product-based brands
	Interest in furniture, home goods, interior design, or lifestyle brands


Who We&#39;re Looking For
Someone who is:


	Proactive and creative
	Commercially minded and results-oriented
	Comfortable working in a startup environment, often evolving priorities
	Genuinely interested in helping build a growing Nigerian brand
	An ideal candidate should be comfortable taking initiative, solving problems independently, and contributing beyond routine task execution.


Work Structure (Hybrid)&nbsp;

Workfrom home

&nbsp;


	Monday: Weekly planning and team alignment meeting
	Tuesday: Content production and marketing activities
	Wed &ndash; Thu: Physical collaboration and execution (on-site)
	Fri &ndash; Sat: Flexible execution and reporting


How Success Is Measured


	Lead generation and conversion rate
	Campaign performance
	Contribution to sales targets
	Content quality and consistency
	Initiative and ownership


Why Join DIY Africans?


	Direct, visible contribution to business growth
	Exposure to digital commerce and brand building
	Close working relationship with company leadership
	Career growth in a fast-growing company
	The chance to shape the digital presence of a Nigerian consumer brand


Salary
N180,000 - N220,000 Month.",

Digital Marketing Executive Job at DIY Africans




        
    

        
        DIY Africans is a Nigerian furniture and home goods company creating functional, affordable, and locally manufactured products for the next generation of African households. 

We combine local manufacturing, digital commerce, and design-led products to make quality home goods more accessible. As we enter our next phase of growth, we're hiring a Digital Marketing Executive to drive awareness, generate leads, support sales, and strengthen our digital presence.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Digital Marketing Executive

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The Opportunity
     

            
    




	As the Digital Marketing Executive you will own DIY Africans' marketing efforts from strategy through execution and operate the role with a commercial marketing mindset with an understanding that marketing drives business growth.
	Confidently planning campaigns, creating content, analyzing performance, and optimizing results in a fast-paced startup environment.


Success looks like:


	Increased brand visibility and a steady flow of qualified leads
	Stronger customer acquisition and online sales growth
	A more consistent, recognizable digital presence
	Product and lifestyle content that reflects the brand's quality
	Reporting: You will work directly with leadership rather than into an existing marketing team - ideal for someone who wants ownership early.


Key Responsibilities
Digital Marketing & Growth:


	Plan and execute digital marketing campaigns across paid and organic channels
	Manage and optimize Meta advertising campaigns (Facebook & Instagram)
	Generate and nurture sales leads through to conversion
	Monitor campaign performance and recommend improvements


Content Creation & Social Media:


	Develop engaging content for social media platforms
	Participate in product and lifestyle content shoots
	Build content calendars and posting schedules
	Manage day-to-day social media activity, including customer enquiries and engagement


Brand Building


	Strengthen DIY Africans' digital presence
	Contribute ideas for brand growth and customer retention


Reporting & Analytics


	Track marketing performance metrics
	Prepare weekly and monthly reports
	Monitor lead generation and conversion performance
	Provide insights and recommendations to leadership


Requirements
Must Have


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 2–4 years' experience in Digital Marketing, Growth Marketing, or Social Media Management.
	Hands-on experience managing Meta Ads (Facebook & Instagram), with budget accountability
	Strong content creation skills
	Good understanding of customer acquisition and lead generation
	Familiarity with Canva, CapCut, or similar creative tools
	Strong written and verbal communication skills


Preferred:


	SEO knowledge
	Google Business Profile experience
	Basic website management experience
	Experience working with product-based brands
	Interest in furniture, home goods, interior design, or lifestyle brands


Who We're Looking For
Someone who is:


	Proactive and creative
	Commercially minded and results-oriented
	Comfortable working in a startup environment, often evolving priorities
	Genuinely interested in helping build a growing Nigerian brand
	An ideal candidate should be comfortable taking initiative, solving problems independently, and contributing beyond routine task execution.


Work Structure (Hybrid) 

Workfrom home

 


	Monday: Weekly planning and team alignment meeting
	Tuesday: Content production and marketing activities
	Wed – Thu: Physical collaboration and execution (on-site)
	Fri – Sat: Flexible execution and reporting


How Success Is Measured


	Lead generation and conversion rate
	Campaign performance
	Contribution to sales targets
	Content quality and consistency
	Initiative and ownership


Why Join DIY Africans?


	Direct, visible contribution to business growth
	Exposure to digital commerce and brand building
	Close working relationship with company leadership
	Career growth in a fast-growing company
	The chance to shape the digital presence of a Nigerian consumer brand


Salary
N180,000 - N220,000 Month.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Hotel Manager",
  "description": "Golden Six Hotel &amp; Restaurant is one of the most preferred hotel in Nigeria and West Africa as a whole and this is all about Hospitality! It is located in Lekki Scheme 1 in the heart of Lagos State, the most economically important state of the country, major financial centre and would be the fifth largest economy in Africa. We offer our corporate clients and walk in guests a perfect blend of relaxation, activities, and African tradition delicately infused to meet the highest international standards.&nbsp;

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Hotel&nbsp;&nbsp;Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Hotel Manager to oversee the daily operations of our upscale hotel.
	The ideal candidate will be a strategic thinker with exceptional leadership skills, a customer-centric approach, and a proven track record in managing a team and ensuring top-notch guest experiences.


Responsibilities


	Provide leadership and guidance to all hotel departments, including front office, housekeeping, food and beverage, sales, and maintenance.
	Monitor and evaluate financial performance, budgeting, and cost control measures to maximize profitability while maintaining quality standards.
	Manage and motivate the hotel staff, fostering a positive&nbsp;&nbsp;work environment and encouraging professional growth and development.
	Oversee guest services, ensuring that guests receive prompt, courteous, and personalized service throughout their stay.
	Maintain high standards of cleanliness, maintenance, and safety throughout the property.
	Develop and maintain relationships with vendors, suppliers, and local businesses to ensure the hotel&#39;s needs are met efficiently.
	Create and implement marketing and promotional strategies to drive occupancy rates and increase revenue.
	Handle guest feedback and complaints in a timely and effective manner, striving to exceed guest expectations.
	Ensure compliance with all legal, health, safety, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	3-5+ years of experience as a Hotel Manager or similar role.
	Strong leadership, organizational, and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent communication abilities, both written and verbal.
	Proficiency in hotel management software and Microsoft Office Suite.
	Strong financial acumen and experience in budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis.
	Exceptional problem-solving and decision-making capabilities.
	Ability to work under pressure and handle multiple tasks simultaneously.
	In-depth knowledge of the local hospitality market and industry trends.


Lekki Market Expectation:
Urban&amp; Regional Planning


	Handling high-end guests including business travelers and expatriates
	Managing boutique or luxury hotel operations
	Ensuring strong security and guest comfort in a busy urban environment


Method of Application
Interestedand qualified candidates should apply by writing and attaching a detailed resume with a valid phone number to: hr@goldensixhotel.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Hotel Manager Job at Golden Six Hotel & Restaurant




        
    

        
        Golden Six Hotel & Restaurant is one of the most preferred hotel in Nigeria and West Africa as a whole and this is all about Hospitality! It is located in Lekki Scheme 1 in the heart of Lagos State, the most economically important state of the country, major financial centre and would be the fifth largest economy in Africa. We offer our corporate clients and walk in guests a perfect blend of relaxation, activities, and African tradition delicately infused to meet the highest international standards. 

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Hotel  Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Hotel Manager to oversee the daily operations of our upscale hotel.
	The ideal candidate will be a strategic thinker with exceptional leadership skills, a customer-centric approach, and a proven track record in managing a team and ensuring top-notch guest experiences.


Responsibilities


	Provide leadership and guidance to all hotel departments, including front office, housekeeping, food and beverage, sales, and maintenance.
	Monitor and evaluate financial performance, budgeting, and cost control measures to maximize profitability while maintaining quality standards.
	Manage and motivate the hotel staff, fostering a positive  work environment and encouraging professional growth and development.
	Oversee guest services, ensuring that guests receive prompt, courteous, and personalized service throughout their stay.
	Maintain high standards of cleanliness, maintenance, and safety throughout the property.
	Develop and maintain relationships with vendors, suppliers, and local businesses to ensure the hotel's needs are met efficiently.
	Create and implement marketing and promotional strategies to drive occupancy rates and increase revenue.
	Handle guest feedback and complaints in a timely and effective manner, striving to exceed guest expectations.
	Ensure compliance with all legal, health, safety, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	3-5+ years of experience as a Hotel Manager or similar role.
	Strong leadership, organizational, and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent communication abilities, both written and verbal.
	Proficiency in hotel management software and Microsoft Office Suite.
	Strong financial acumen and experience in budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis.
	Exceptional problem-solving and decision-making capabilities.
	Ability to work under pressure and handle multiple tasks simultaneously.
	In-depth knowledge of the local hospitality market and industry trends.


Lekki Market Expectation:
Urban& Regional Planning


	Handling high-end guests including business travelers and expatriates
	Managing boutique or luxury hotel operations
	Ensuring strong security and guest comfort in a busy urban environment


Method of Application
Interestedand qualified candidates should apply by writing and attaching a detailed resume with a valid phone number to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Officer",
  "description": "Beyond Clothing is a large-scale garment manufacturing company based in Aba, Nigeria. We produce school uniforms, corporate apparel, and casual clothing for institutions, brands, and organizations that require quality, consistency, and reliability. Our operations combine skilled craftsmanship with structured production systems, ensuring dependable delivery at scale.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location: Aba, Abia
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	An account officer keeps precise financial records, monitors incoming and outgoing funds, and supports the finance department with daily bookkeeping and reporting tasks.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage accounts payable (money the company owes) and accounts receivable (money owed to the company).
	Verify, cross-check, and process vendor and customer invoices.
	Reconcile bank statements, cash accounts, and general ledger balances.
	Contact clients or vendors regarding past-due accounts and billing discrepancies.
	Assist with tax computations, payroll administration, and financial audit preparations.


Education and Experience
Education:


	A Bachelor&#39;s degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.


Experience:


	1 to 3 years of proven working experience in bookkeeping, general accounting, or a corporate finance environment.


Other Requirements:


	High level of personal integrity and professional ethics when handling confidential data.
	Ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work well under pressure and meet tight month-end or tax reporting deadlines
	Mathematical and analytical proficiency.
	High attention to detail and accuracy.


Salary


	N140,000 - N160,000 gross
	Pension contributions, and performance bonuses.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CVs to: info@beyondclothingng.com using &#39;Account Officer&#39; as the subject of the email.

Note: For more information, WhatsApp: 08167600440.",

Account Officer Job at Beyond Clothing




        
    

        
        Beyond Clothing is a large-scale garment manufacturing company based in Aba, Nigeria. We produce school uniforms, corporate apparel, and casual clothing for institutions, brands, and organizations that require quality, consistency, and reliability. Our operations combine skilled craftsmanship with structured production systems, ensuring dependable delivery at scale. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location: Aba, Abia
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	An account officer keeps precise financial records, monitors incoming and outgoing funds, and supports the finance department with daily bookkeeping and reporting tasks.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage accounts payable (money the company owes) and accounts receivable (money owed to the company).
	Verify, cross-check, and process vendor and customer invoices.
	Reconcile bank statements, cash accounts, and general ledger balances.
	Contact clients or vendors regarding past-due accounts and billing discrepancies.
	Assist with tax computations, payroll administration, and financial audit preparations.


Education and Experience
Education:


	A Bachelor's degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.


Experience:


	1 to 3 years of proven working experience in bookkeeping, general accounting, or a corporate finance environment.


Other Requirements:


	High level of personal integrity and professional ethics when handling confidential data.
	Ability to  work well under pressure and meet tight month-end or tax reporting deadlines
	Mathematical and analytical proficiency.
	High attention to detail and accuracy.


Salary


	N140,000 - N160,000 gross
	Pension contributions, and performance bonuses.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CVs to: [email protected] using 'Account Officer' as the subject of the email.

Note: For more information, WhatsApp: 08167600440.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Direct Sales Supervisor",
  "description": "Adia Credit is a consumer loan provider in Nigeria that offers consumers an installment payment package on mobile phones that can be obtained in the store within 60 minutes without collateral.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Direct Sales Supervisor

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Sales Supervisor with experience in BNPL mobile phone sales.
	The Sales Supervisor will be responsible for leading and managing direct sales agents, driving sales performance, ensuring effective customer acquisition, onboard new retail partners acrossLagos and maintaining compliance with BNPL policies.


Responsibilities


	&nbsp;Manage and coach a team of BNPL direct sales agents
	Maintain strong relationships with partnered phone stores
	Explore and onboard new retail partners across Lagos
	Monitor team performance and ensure target delivery
	Assist in dispute resolution and customer satisfaction


Requirements


	Bachelors&rsquo; Degree in any relevant field
	1- 3 years of experience in BNPL mobile phone financing and supervisory role
	Strong leadership, coaching and team management skill
	Excellent communication, negotiation and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target driven environment
	Proficiency in Microsoft office tools and sales reporting.


Remuneration
Competitive salary with performance-based incentive.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@ishiagumfb.ng using the Job Position and location as the subject of the email.",

Direct Sales Supervisor Job at Adia Credit




        
    

        
        Adia Credit is a consumer loan provider in Nigeria that offers consumers an installment payment package on mobile phones that can be obtained in the store within 60 minutes without collateral.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Direct Sales Supervisor

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Sales Supervisor with experience in BNPL mobile phone sales.
	The Sales Supervisor will be responsible for leading and managing direct sales agents, driving sales performance, ensuring effective customer acquisition, onboard new retail partners acrossLagos and maintaining compliance with BNPL policies.


Responsibilities


	 Manage and coach a team of BNPL direct sales agents
	Maintain strong relationships with partnered phone stores
	Explore and onboard new retail partners across Lagos
	Monitor team performance and ensure target delivery
	Assist in dispute resolution and customer satisfaction


Requirements


	Bachelors’ Degree in any relevant field
	1- 3 years of experience in BNPL mobile phone financing and supervisory role
	Strong leadership, coaching and team management skill
	Excellent communication, negotiation and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target driven environment
	Proficiency in Microsoft office tools and sales reporting.


Remuneration
Competitive salary with performance-based incentive.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and location as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Treasury Manager Job at PPC Limited




        
    

        
        PPC (formerly Philips Projects Centre) is a wholly Nigerian-owned multi-competency engineering and infrastructure development company. Its core activities are primarily geared towards providing specialized end-to-end services for projects in ICT, Power, Healthcare, Transportation, Building Services and other utility sectors.
     

            
    



Commencing operations in 1991 as Philips Projects Centre, an affiliate of Philips Electronics N.V. of the Netherlands. The PPC acronym was retained after a strategic management buyout of the Dutch parent company. Thus, with this strategic buyout, the old businesses were retained, with the added advantage of the exclusive distributorship of the numerous professional Philips products and the provision of integrated solutions based on cutting-edge technology.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Treasury  Manager 

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Treasury Management:


	Responsible for monitoring the availability of funds, monitoring all bank balances, and requesting funds transfers when necessary.
	Responsible for preparing monthly and revised weekly cash forecasts.
	Prepare monthly variance analyses of actual cash collection against forecast.
	Liaison with banks on all cash and related matters.
	Responsible for reviewing cheque vouchers.
	Supervise daily Cash count.
	Responsible for preparing weekly bank position statement.
	Responsible for conducting regular cash audit.
	Responsible for processing documents required for foreign transactions.
	Any other related tasks assigned by management.


Portfolio Management:


	Responsible for processing investment of idle funds with banks.
	Responsible for preparing and maintaining a schedule of interest on invested funds.
	Responsible for maintaining proper records of investment certificates.
	Any other related tasks assigned by management.


Requirements


	Education - BSc. Accounting or Equivalent.
	Professional Qualification:
	
		ICAN
		ACCA.
	
	
	Experience - 5-7 years.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
